title: Health & Emergencies
shortSummary: Describe symptoms, see a doctor, and get help fast in an emergency.
culturalNote: Germany has a strong healthcare system, and pharmacies (Apotheke), marked with a red A, are everywhere and staffed by knowledgeable pharmacists who can advise on minor ailments. The pan-European emergency number is 112 for ambulance and fire, and 110 for police. On Sundays and at night, a rota of Notdienst (emergency duty) pharmacies stays open.

grammar.explanation: German builds many ailments by adding -schmerzen (pains) to a body part: Kopfschmerzen (headache), Bauchschmerzen (stomach ache), Halsschmerzen (sore throat). Use them with haben: 'Ich habe Zahnschmerzen'.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying 'Ich bin Kopfschmerzen'. Use haben: 'Ich habe Kopfschmerzen'.

grammar.explanation: Some feelings use a dative construction with no 'I' as subject: 'Mir ist kalt' (I am cold), 'Mir ist schlecht' (I feel sick). Literally: to-me is cold.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying 'Ich bin schlecht' (which means 'I am a bad person'). Use 'Mir ist schlecht'.

dialogue.setup: Lena visits a pharmacy because she is not feeling well.

lines.speaker: Apotheker
lines.german: Guten Tag, was kann ich für Sie tun?
lines.english: Hello, what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.german: Ich fühle mich nicht gut. Ich habe Kopfschmerzen und Fieber.
lines.english: I don't feel well. I have a headache and a fever.

lines.speaker: Apotheker
lines.german: Seit wann haben Sie die Symptome?
lines.english: Since when have you had the symptoms?

lines.speaker: Apotheker
lines.german: Gut. Nehmen Sie diese Tabletten und ruhen Sie sich aus. Gute Besserung!
lines.english: Good. Take these tablets and rest. Get well soon!

quiz.question: How do you say 'I have a headache'?
Which sentence means 'I feel sick'?
quiz.explanation: Feeling sick uses the dative: 'Mir ist schlecht'.

quiz.question: What do you say to someone who is unwell?
Arrange: einen / brauche / Arzt / ich
quiz.explanation: Subject ich, verb brauche, then the accusative object 'einen Arzt'.
